title: Reason category catalogue
description: Five canonical reason categories that group per-network reason codes. Used by rate reports + fraud model signals + evidence collection strategy. Each category has typical dispute rate + evidence-defense hints.

categories.description: Cardholder claims the transaction was unauthorized — the card was stolen / cloned / used without permission. Highest fraud correlation signal for ad-network fraud model retraining.
evidence_defense_hints: Customer signature evidence (checkout signature + IP + device fingerprint hash)
Prior purchase history from same customer (CE3.0-eligible on Visa)
IP geolocation matching customer's billing address
Device fingerprint matching prior legitimate purchases
categories.notes: Fraud chargebacks feed ad-network fraud model retraining MORE HEAVILY than other categories. Ad networks reduce bidding on click IDs correlated with fraud chargebacks. CE3.0 evidence framework (Visa) dramatically improves win rates when applicable.

categories.description: Auth issues — transaction was declined but processed anyway, card was expired, no authorization was obtained. Merchant liability.
categories.notes: Rare category. Almost always merchant fault (declined-then-processed, expired-card processing, offline forcing). Hard to win — evidence typically doesn't help when the technical fault is on merchant side.

categories.description: Merchant processed the transaction incorrectly — duplicate charge, wrong amount, wrong currency, credit not processed, late presentment.
evidence_defense_hints: Transaction detail records showing correct amount / currency / timing
categories.notes: Often winnable with clear transaction detail records. Some codes (duplicate_processing) always favor cardholder — resolve via refund instead of fighting.

categories.description: Cardholder received the goods/services but has a service quality issue — not as described, defective, not received, cancelled subscription still charged.
evidence_defense_hints: Service documentation (attendance records for fitness academies, delivery confirmation for goods)
Communication log (customer support conversations, terms acceptance)
Refund policy snapshot at purchase time
Cancellation flow records (proves cardholder DID NOT cancel prior to billing)
categories.notes: Product-quality signal for ad networks — feeds bidding adjustments on customer segments with high dispute rates, but does NOT aggressively retrain fraud models. Winnable with strong service documentation.

categories.description: Special card status (card recovery bulletin, hot card list). Rare — cardholder or issuer flagged the card as compromised but merchant processed anyway.
evidence_defense_hints: Authorization records showing the card was NOT flagged at time of transaction
Timing evidence — did the card recovery bulletin arrive before or after our transaction
categories.notes: Very rare. Almost always resolves via issuer records showing the flag was in place at transaction time.

notes: typical_dispute_rate_percent is INFORMATIONAL — real rates vary by industry + tenant. Used for report benchmarking + admin education.
evidence_defense_hints inform the SDUI evidence-submission wizard (suggests what to include per category).
win_rate_baseline_percent is INFORMATIONAL — real win rates depend on evidence quality + reason code specifics. Enterprise-tier customers may see higher win rates with CE3.0 evidence.
marketing_signal_weight informs the marketing::ChargebackFiled event's fraud_signal flag semantics + weight passed to ad networks.
